images.copyright: Polar bear mother and cubs playing in Wapusk National Park, Manitoba, Canada (© Hao Jiang/Getty Images)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Polar bears in Wapusk National Park, Manitoba, Canada
images.desc: Captured in Wapusk National Park in northern Manitoba, Canada, today's image shows a polar bear mother and her cubs sharing a playful moment on the Arctic tundra. For polar bears, motherhood is a long and demanding commitment. Females give birth in winter, sheltering their newborn cubs in snow dens that provide vital insulation. Cubs are born tiny and rely entirely on their mother's rich, fat-heavy milk for warmth and rapid growth.

images.copyright: Skradinski Buk Waterfall in Krka National Park, Croatia (© Amazing Aerial Agency/Adobe Stock)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Skradinski Buk Waterfall, Krka National Park, Croatia
images.desc: Water is the architect of Krka National Park in Croatia, shaping emerald pools and layered waterfalls along the Krka River. Today's image highlights Skradinski Buk, the park's most famous waterfall system, where multiple streams spill over travertine terraces in a dense forest near the village of Lozovac.

images.title: Info
images.caption: Sardinian donkey mare and foal, France
images.desc: Few animals have matched humanity's quiet resilience quite like the donkey. Donkeys have worked alongside people for thousands of years, prized for their endurance, sharp memory and sure-footed confidence on rugged ground.

images.copyright: Kofa National Wildlife Refuge, Arizona, United States (© Denis Tangney Jr/Getty Images)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Kofa National Wildlife Refuge, Arizona, United States
images.desc: Stretching across the Sonoran Desert in southwestern Arizona, Kofa National Wildlife Refuge is a land of sharp peaks, wide skies and surprising life. The refuge was established in 1939 to protect desert bighorn sheep, whose sure-footed silhouettes still define the rugged landscape.

images.title: Info
images.caption: Thunderstorm above the plains, Bulgaria
images.desc: Located on the eastern Balkan Peninsula, Bulgaria sits at the crossroads of Europe and Asia, where ancient cultures, fertile landscapes and shifting climates converge.

images.copyright: Field of blue agave near Tequila, Jalisco, Mexico (© Brian Overcast/Alamy)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Field of blue agave near Tequila, Jalisco, Mexico
images.desc: In the highlands of Jalisco, Mexico, rows of blue agave stretch across the landscape, their spiky leaves catching the light like a field of sculpted stars. This region, centred around the town of Tequila, is the birthplace of tequila, a spirit deeply tied to both land and tradition.

images.copyright: Ksar Ouled Soltane, Tataouine district in southern Tunisia (© Dark_Eni/Getty Images Plus)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Ksar Ouled Soltane, Tataouine district in southern Tunisia
images.desc: Today's image takes us to Ksar Ouled Soltane in Tunisia's Tataouine district, where stacked clay granaries rise like sand-coloured fortresses. Dating back to the 15th century, this storehouse complex was built by Berber communities to protect harvests from heat, theft and time. Function first. Beauty as a side effect.

images.copyright: Leopard sleeping in a tree in the savannah, Masai Mara National Reserve, Kenya (© Klein & Hubert/Nature Picture Library)
images.title: Info
images.caption: Leopard sleeping in a tree in Masai Mara National Reserve, Kenya
images.desc: High above the savannah in Kenya's Masai Mara National Reserve, a leopard curls up on a tree branch, napping as the grasslands glow at sunset. Today, let's throw light on one of the world's most adaptable big cats. Leopards roam across Africa and parts of Asia, thriving in habitats that range from forests and mountains to open savannas. Their spotted coats are unique to each individual, like fingerprints, and their powerful climbing skills allow them to stash prey high in trees, out of reach of scavengers.
